subagent_reports.topic: Modeling/querying lightweight dependency/relationship state in Temporal TS: workflow state vs search attributes/memo tradeoffs

sources.label: Temporal docs — Visibility Search Attributes (encyclopedia)
sources.summary: Search Attributes are for search/collection result-sets, NOT business logic. For business logic about a workflow, store state in a local variable + expose via Query, or store in external datastore via Activities. Custom SA limits vary by Visibility DB (SQLite/MySQL/PG ~3 per most types incl KeywordList; Cloud 20 Bool/Datetime/Double/Int, 5 KeywordList/Text, 40 Keyword; ES unbounded; none on Cassandra). Custom SA in List Filters require advanced Visibility + Server v1.20+ for SQL DBs (else Elasticsearch).

sources.label: Temporal docs — TypeScript observability (upsertSearchAttributes)
sources.summary: upsertSearchAttributes() inside workflow add/update/delete SA; read via workflowInfo().searchAttributes. Empty array deletes an attribute.

sources.label: Temporal docs — list-filter (Text/Keyword matching semantics)
sources.summary: Text SA tokenized (any-token match). Keyword/KeywordList exact-value match — required for reliable collision/uniqueness equality filters.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Memo (client options)
sources.summary: Memo is arbitrary non-indexed key-value metadata attached at start. Returned in list results but NOT queryable in List Filters. Good for descriptive payload, not for filtering/collision detection.

sources.label: Temporal docs — TypeScript message passing (Query)
sources.summary: defineQuery/setHandler: synchronous, must not mutate state, no async. Strongly-consistent read of in-workflow state — correct primitive for authoritative dependency-graph reads on a known workflow.

sources.label: Local — plugin/src/temporal/search-attributes.ts
sources.summary: ADV already uses typed-state→Visibility projection: buildChangeSearchAttributes() maps ChangeWorkflowState to SA; KeywordList capped at 3 (AdvAffectedProjects/AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths) due to dev-server limit; AdvBacklogIssueNumber single Keyword for cross-session claim-collision query (rq-backlogCoord01). Header comment documents the 3-KeywordList hard cap.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: ADV's existing typed-state→Visibility pattern is canonical and by-the-book. Temporal docs explicitly direct: Search Attributes for search/collection queries; workflow-local state + Query for business logic; external store via Activities for high-throughput/low-latency. ADV correctly keeps per-change workflow state as source of truth (signal/query) and projects a derived subset into Visibility SA for cross-change discovery. For a lightweight dependency/relationship graph the boring/correct split is: (a) authoritative edges in typed workflow state, queried via defineQuery on the owning workflow; (b) only the minimal indexable keys (e.g. issue number, branch, parent-change id) projected to Keyword/KeywordList SA for cross-workflow collision/relationship discovery. Memo is unsuitable for filtering (non-queryable). The binding constraint is the KeywordList per-namespace cap (3 on SQLite/dev-server, 5 on Cloud) — already exhausted (3/3). A new KeywordList-typed dependency attribute would NOT fit without displacing an existing one; encode multi-value relationships as a single joined Keyword, or as a single-valued Keyword (parent id) rather than a new KeywordList.

validation.notes: No anti-pattern in the existing approach. Caveats are real and must shape design: (1) KeywordList budget exhausted on dev-server/SQLite (3/3) — header comment in search-attributes.ts already warns; (2) Visibility is eventually consistent — there is lag between state change and Visibility update, so SA-based collision/uniqueness checks are advisory/best-effort, not strongly consistent (consistent with P33 structural-correctness: do not make Visibility the sole authority for collision gating); (3) custom-SA List Filters need advanced Visibility + Server v1.20+ on SQL DBs.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Model dependency/relationship edges in typed ChangeWorkflowState (authoritative, query via defineQuery), and project ONLY a minimal discovery key into Visibility. Prefer a single-valued Keyword (e.g. parent/blocking change id) over a new KeywordList — the 3-slot KeywordList budget is already full on the dev-server/SQLite Visibility store. Treat any Visibility-based collision/uniqueness check as advisory due to eventual consistency; enforce true uniqueness/ordering structurally in workflow state or via an authoritative Query/Activity, not via a Visibility list query alone. Do not use memo for any queryable relationship data (non-filterable).
follow_ups: Confirm the deployed Visibility store + Temporal Server version for this project (dev-server/SQLite vs Cloud/ES) — it sets the real KeywordList cap (3 vs 5) and whether advanced-Visibility List Filters are available.

If a new multi-value dependency dimension is truly needed and KeywordList budget is full, decide between joined-single-Keyword encoding vs displacing an existing KeywordList (e.g. AdvWorktreePaths) — needs a usage audit of current SA queries.
lgrep semantic search timed out twice (hybrid + non-hybrid); local refs gathered via grep/read instead. Re-index recommended for future semantic queries.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Visibility Search Attributes + Query (prior researcher report, this change)
sources.summary: Authoritative business-logic relationships belong in workflow state read via defineQuery; Search Attributes are advisory/eventually-consistent discovery keys; memo is non-queryable. Confirms edge graph must live in typed ChangeWorkflowState, not Visibility.

sources.summary: adv_status/change_list already have read-path/full-hydration cost concerns; any new lineage/ops read surface must use lazy/summary projection, not full change hydration.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The approved scope is well-served by EXTENDING existing typed-state primitives rather than introducing a parallel ops-job subsystem. Four boring leverage points already exist in-tree: (1) the advisory ExternalDependency pattern (non-blocking, typed relationship enum) is the canonical precedent for 'release-first follow-up with surviving obligation' — the proposal's hardest requirement; (2) cross-project follow-up already maintains a bidirectional edge (source.cross_project_links write-back), giving a same-codebase template for adding the missing same-project parent->children reverse edge; (3) the required-follow-up agenda promotion path is the single chokepoint where typed provenance is lost to free text — adding typed source/relationship fields there is high-payoff and low-blast-radius; (4) Temporal guidance + the exhausted KeywordList budget jointly force the edge graph into typed workflow state with at most one single-valued Keyword discovery key, which matches ADV's existing pattern. The main risk is vocabulary fragmentation: a THIRD relationship enum (block/follow/monitor/cleanup-after/teardown-after) would collide conceptually with the two existing enums; discovery should decide whether ops edges extend ExternalDependencyRelationship or introduce a deliberately distinct, documented ops-relationship enum.

validation.notes: No anti-pattern found in the proposed direction. All five candidates are in-scope, evidence-backed, and consistent with prior considerations (release-first sequencing, lazy read paths, exhausted SA budget). Candidate 1 (release-first via advisory edge) and Candidate 3 (typed promotion provenance) are the highest-leverage; both reuse existing primitives. Two items are flagged user-surface (relationship vocabulary unification, wip_state lineage exposure) because they involve a design tradeoff the orchestrator/user should adjudicate, not auto-adopt.

subagent_reports.recommendation: In discovery, anchor the model on existing typed primitives: (a) reuse the advisory/non-blocking ExternalDependency shape as the template for release-first surviving obligations; (b) add the missing same-project parent->children reverse edge using the cross_project_links write-back as a template; (c) make the subagent-report->agenda promotion path carry typed source_change + relationship + contract refs instead of the free-text 'Source:' line; (d) keep the edge graph authoritative in ChangeWorkflowState (query-read) with at most one single-valued Keyword discovery key — do NOT request a new KeywordList. Surface the relationship-vocabulary decision (extend ExternalDependencyRelationship vs new ops enum) and the wip_state lineage-exposure choice to the user as design tradeoffs.
follow_ups: lgrep semantic index was stale/timed out in the prior attempt for this change; local evidence gathered via grep/read. Re-index recommended before next semantic query.
Discovery must decide relationship-enum strategy (reuse vs new) to avoid a third divergent vocabulary across CrossProjectLink/ExternalDependency/ops-follow-up.
Confirm whether adv_wip_state lineage exposure should be summary-only (id + parent/origin id) to honor the lazy read-path constraint from docs/repo-improve-prep.md.

sources.label: Local — subagent-report.ts consumeRequiredFollowUps + reportSourceDescription
sources.summary: required_follow_ups already carry obligation_class + source_contract_id provenance and are written to agenda items with category 'required-obligation' (priority critical/standard) via addAgendaItem. This is the exact free-text 'agenda archaeology' pipe the change wants to replace; the promotion tool should consume this same typed RequiredFollowUp object as its structural source.

sources.label: Local — prep-readiness.ts checkCriticalOpsCoverage (rq-PR007coc)
sources.summary: The agreement's 'enforceCriticalOpsPlanning' does not exist by that name; the real blocker is checkCriticalOpsCoverage, which blocks planning when a requiredCritical contract item lacks task coverage (implements/verifies) unless notRequiredReason set. Design correctly preserves it as the hard-blocking path, but AC #9 names a non-existent symbol.

sources.label: Local — changes.ts existing relationship schemas
sources.summary: Three relationship vocabularies already exist: CrossProjectLinkRelationship (origin/follow_up/coordinates_with/depends_on), ExternalDependencyRelationship (requires/blocks/coordinates_with, advisory:literal(true) = intentionally non-blocking), FastFollowOf (child->parent only, no reverse edge). Design adds a 4th ops relationship enum (blocks/follows_release/monitors/cleanup_after).

sources.label: Local — ExternalDependencySchema advisory:literal(true)
sources.summary: ExternalDependency is V1 advisory-only (non-blocking by construction). It already models 'blocks' as a relationship label that does NOT actually block. Reusing this primitive for ops links would conflict with the design's requirement that ops 'blocks' must actually gate release.

sources.label: Prior researcher report (attached to change) — Temporal Visibility/SA tradeoffs
sources.summary: Confirms: typed workflow state authoritative + single Keyword projection is canonical; KeywordList budget 3/3 exhausted on dev-server SQLite; Visibility eventually consistent so collision/handoff checks must enforce structurally, not via SA list query. Design's Decision #4 (Visibility discovery-only) and Design-Derived Criteria #1/#2 already align.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is structurally sound and by-the-book: typed workflow state owns correctness, signals mutate, queries/read-tools expose, Visibility is discovery-only. The single highest-leverage gap is that the design treats the promotion tool as a fresh 'agent retypes agenda text' problem, when in fact required_follow_ups already arrive as TYPED objects (RequiredFollowUp with obligation_class + source_contract_id) and are merely lossily flattened into agenda free-text by consumeRequiredFollowUps. Hooking adv_followup_promote into that existing typed object — not the agenda string — is the boring, proven adopt path and directly satisfies AC #2 (free-text agenda not the sole source of truth). Secondary leverage: the design proliferates a 4th relationship enum and a parallel ops_followup_links array beside three existing linkage primitives; the campsite-correct move is a single ops relationship enum that the existing cross-project/fast-follow/external-dependency relationships MAP INTO for readback (design risk table already names this), rather than net-new parallel machinery. Watch-out: ExternalDependency.advisory:literal(true) means its 'blocks' label is non-blocking — do not reuse that primitive for ops 'blocks' that must gate release; that is a design_around, not an adopt.

validation.notes: No release/security blockers. Two design-shaping cautions: (1) AC #9 cites 'enforceCriticalOpsPlanning' which does not exist — the real preserved blocker is checkCriticalOpsCoverage (rq-PR007coc); planning should reference the correct symbol so the regression test target is unambiguous. (2) The reverse-link write-after-create partial-failure risk (design risk table row 4) is real because FastFollowOf is child->parent only and reverse edges are new; structural repair-from-child-provenance must be specced, not left to retry hope.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt: route adv_followup_promote off the existing typed RequiredFollowUp object (obligation_class + source_contract_id), reusing addAgendaItem's provenance pattern but writing a typed linked change instead of agenda free-text. Design-around: keep a SINGLE ops relationship enum and map existing CrossProjectLink/ExternalDependency/FastFollowOf relationships into it for readback rather than adding a 4th parallel vocabulary; do NOT reuse ExternalDependency (advisory:literal(true)) for ops blocks that must gate release. Surface: correct AC #9 to name checkCriticalOpsCoverage (rq-PR007coc) and spec the reverse-link repair path. Follow_up: confirm whether the parent-side ops_followup_links should subsume or coexist with cross_project_links to avoid dual-write drift.
follow_ups: Planning: replace AC #9 symbol 'enforceCriticalOpsPlanning' with the actual checkCriticalOpsCoverage (rq-PR007coc) and add a regression test asserting requiredCritical coverage still blocks.
Design: decide single-enum-with-mapping vs net-new ops_followup_links array; if coexisting with cross_project_links, define dual-write ownership to avoid drift.
Design: spec reverse-link partial-failure repair from child provenance (FastFollowOf is currently child->parent only, no reverse edge exists today).
Confirm deployed Visibility store/server version to fix the real KeywordList cap (3 dev-server vs 5 Cloud) before any single-Keyword ops discovery projection is added.

sources.summary: RequiredFollowUp / required_follow_ups[] with obligation_class+severity+source_contract_id are typed persisted report fields; follow_ups become bounded source-tagged agenda items. sources.label: Temporal official docs: Memo vs Search Attributes
sources.summary: Memo is non-indexed metadata; Search Attributes are indexed and queryable via List Filters. Canonically confirms design avoidance 'no Temporal Memo for filterable relationship/collision data' — Memo cannot back collision/relationship filters.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is structurally sound and extends established ADV patterns (typed change-workflow state + signal/query + Visibility projection) rather than introducing a parallel project-manager object — directly honoring the 'no PM clone' constraint. All material reuse claims are source-verified: ExternalDependency is advisory-only (correctly NOT reused for blocking), fast_follow_of is child->parent only (reverse-link gap real), KeywordList budget is genuinely full (3/3), and Memo is non-indexed per official Temporal docs (correctly excluded from collision filtering). Objectives map to concrete mechanisms: durable provenance (typed OpsFollowupProfile/Link), release-first handoff (relationship enum + explicit handoff record), light evidence (append-only OpsEvidenceEntry), and structural WIP visibility (read-surface annotations + minimal Keyword). Two non-blocking gaps: (1) AC#9 / design name the enforcement symbol inconsistently — agreement says 'enforceCriticalOpsPlanning', actual+design say 'checkCriticalOpsCoverage'; it gates PLANNING not release, so framing it as preserving 'release-safety' enforcement is slightly imprecise but harmless. (2) Design leaves OpsFollowupLink storage authority ambiguous (authoritative typed state vs derived from child profile) — acceptable for design phase but planning must pick one to keep correctness structural (P33) and avoid dual-write drift.

validation.notes: VALIDATED with minor CAUTIONS. No spec-law conflict found. (1) Symbol-name drift: agreement AC#9 'enforceCriticalOpsPlanning' does not exist; real symbol is checkCriticalOpsCoverage (prep-readiness.ts:433) and gates planning, not release — design cites it correctly; reconcile agreement wording at planning. (2) OpsFollowupLink authoritative-vs-derived storage left open — resolve in planning to one source of truth (structural correctness, repairable reverse link already specified). (3) Simpler alternative considered: reuse ExternalDependency for links — correctly rejected by design because advisory:literal(true) is non-blocking and cannot carry the 'blocks' release semantic; introducing OpsRelationship as the single read-model vocabulary is the right call and avoids a 4th uncoordinated enum. No materially simpler viable approach overlooked.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to planning. Verdict VALIDATED with two minor cautions to resolve during /adv-plan: (a) reconcile the AC#9 enforcement-symbol naming to checkCriticalOpsCoverage and clarify it is the PLANNING-gate required-critical guard (not a release guard), so the new release-first handoff logic is additive and does not claim to extend that function; (b) commit to ONE authoritative storage model for OpsFollowupLink (typed workflow state preferred for structural-correctness P33, with derived projection for reads) and keep the reverse-link-repair-from-child-provenance path as the failure mode. Reuse decisions (reject ExternalDependency for blocking links, single Keyword for discovery, no Memo for filterable data, structural WIP over agenda scan) are all source-backed and should be carried forward unchanged.
follow_ups: Planning: reconcile agreement AC#9 symbol name to checkCriticalOpsCoverage and clarify planning-gate vs release-gate scope.
Planning: choose authoritative vs derived storage for OpsFollowupLink to avoid dual-write drift.
Planning: confirm OpsRelationship->existing-enum mapping table (CrossProjectLink follow_up/depends_on, ExternalDependency blocks/requires, fast_follow_of) is exhaustive and lossless for read model.

wisdom.content: For Temporal-backed ADV state extensions, keep authority split explicit in types and tests: workflow state owns correctness, display snapshots are non-authoritative, signals are idempotent by structural IDs, and legacy optional-field normalization prevents old changes from failing strict parsing.

For follow-up promotion features, keep promotion as a thin dedicated tool that wraps existing change creation and fires typed workflow signals; preserve source identity structurally (report/change/scope/attempt/contract/agenda) and make duplicate detection use that identity, not title similarity.

For evidence append tools, expose a caller-friendly operational status enum but map structurally to separate internal evidence-entry and aggregate-profile statuses; generate provenance fields (id, recorded_at) inside the tool and reject blank required evidence fields before signaling.

For new change metadata that must appear in hot read paths, extend ChangeSummary/list projections first and share a compact projection helper across adv_change_list/adv_wip_state; this keeps readback structural without artifact hydration or duplicated display logic.

For release blockers, keep blocker metadata structural in GateReadinessBlocker (linkId/changeId/relationship) and separately expose non-blocking open obligations in archive/release reports; this distinguishes hard gates from surviving handoff context without message parsing.

documents.proposal:
# Add Ops Follow-up Traceability

## Why

ADV lacks a first-class way to preserve important ops/enabler follow-ups as durable linked lifecycle obligations. When migrations, backfills, monitoring runs, deploy-config work, cleanup, or teardown steps become agenda notes, agents lose reliable traceability for what started, what environment it ran in, what batch/evidence exists, what remains, and how it relates to parent changes. ADV needs lightweight but structured linked ops/follow-up state so agents can create, find, reason about, and complete this work without manual bookkeeping or agenda archaeology.

## What Changes

1. Add durable traceability for ops/enabler follow-ups that need ADV-level rigor but may not fit ordinary code-task flow.
2. Model explicit relationship edges between originating changes and follow-up work, including cases where the parent must release before the follow-up can complete.
3. Preserve lightweight operational evidence for runs, batches, environments, validation, cleanup, and next steps.
4. Make active ops/enabler follow-up work visible to agents during planning and collision checks.
5. Preserve existing required-obligation enforcement while adding better routing for post-release or ops-native obligations.

## User Outcomes

- Users can trust agents to find active ops/enabler work without searching agenda notes manually.
- Users can see how ops follow-ups relate to originating changes and whether they block, follow, monitor, or clean up after those changes.
- Users can release parent changes when release-first sequencing is necessary while retaining durable linked obligations.
- Users can review a concise operational record showing what ran, where it ran, what evidence was captured, what remains, and when completion is proven.
- Users can rely on planning-time checks to surface active related ops work before agents start conflicting changes.

## Affected Code

Likely affected surfaces, pending discovery:

- Change metadata and Temporal workflow state for linked follow-up/ops relationships.
- Change creation paths for same-project fast-follow and cross-project origin linkage.
- Sub-agent report ingestion and required follow-up consumers.
- Agenda/follow-up promotion paths.
- Status, WIP, and planning/collision read paths.
- Gate readiness or release handoff surfaces where unresolved linked obligations must be surfaced.
- Specs covering advance workflow, sub-agent reports, prep readiness, and backlog/WIP coordination.

## Related Repositories

- Current repository: `advance`
- No product-linked secondary repo identified in `project.json`.

## Constraints

- Do not turn ADV into a project-manager clone.
- Do not make every linked ops follow-up a release blocker.
- Do not rely on agenda scanning as the source of truth.
- Do not require active human bookkeeping.
- Do not make the model infra-specific; it must cover migrations, backfills, deploy config, monitoring, cleanup, teardown, docs, and similar enablers.
- Preserve structural correctness: typed state and validated transitions over title/priority heuristics.
- Preserve existing human checkpoints and approval boundaries.
- Keep the operational evidence record light but durable.
- Discovery must define how failed, partial, rerun-needed, or rollback/cleanup-needed ops evidence is represented.

## Impact

- Reduces hidden follow-up risk after releases.
- Improves agent ability to resume, monitor, or complete operational work.
- Gives planning workflows a durable source for related active ops obligations.
- Clarifies when parent changes can release with surviving linked follow-up obligations.
- May require new typed state, visibility/search attributes, and UI/status readback for linked work.

## Context

Current ADV has partial pieces:

- Same-project `fast_follow_of` lineage via `parent_change_id`.
- Cross-project origin linkage via `source_change_id`.
- Change origins via `origin.kind` and `origin_source_artifact`.
- Required follow-ups from worker reports becoming required-obligation agenda items.
- Existing required-obligation enforcement for in-scope release-safety work.

These pieces do not yet provide an ops-native durable record with dependency semantics, run/batch evidence, environment, cleanup/teardown steps, completion proof, or planning-time collision visibility. PokeEdge examples show frequent linked data/import/image/CN follow-ups where release-first sequencing is often valid; therefore a simple always-block-parent rule is not sufficient.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Result | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Skills Considered | PASS | Loaded `adv-agent-tool-contracts`, `adv-worktree`, and `adv-opportunity-scout`; no pending-review skill found. |
| Extends | PASS | Cited `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`; new finding: ops-link read paths should avoid full-hydration hot paths and prefer summary/query projections. |
| Conflict Scan | PASS with degraded archive list | Active list found no conflicting active change except this change; archived list timed out, but known archived `enforceCriticalOpsPlanning` was inspected and is adjacent not duplicate. `adv_change_validate` passed with expected pre-prep warnings. Agenda has many subagent follow-ups, confirming agenda visibility pressure. |
| Edge Cases | PASS | Edge cases listed below. |
| Open Design Questions | PASS | Design questions listed below with trust model, blast radius, alternatives. |
| Draft Spec Deltas | PASS | Draft deltas listed below. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Current patterns found across fast-follow, cross-project origin, external dependencies, required follow-ups, and backlog claim visibility. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Long-term best practice: typed workflow state owns edges; Visibility projects minimal query keys; memo is not used for filterable relationship data. |
| Completeness Verification | PASS | Problem spans several existing surfaces; no sole-entry claim remains unverified. Secondary surfaces are classified below. |

### Skills Considered

- `adv-agent-tool-contracts`: relevant because likely changes touch Zod/tool schemas, worker report payloads, and agent-callable tool contracts.
- `adv-worktree`: considered but not directly applied during discovery; future implementation work remains worktree-isolated.
- `adv-opportunity-scout`: triggered due strategic workflow/state design; scout produced five candidates, four auto-adopted as low-risk contract-tied opportunities and one surfaced for user clarification.
- No agent-created pending-review skills found; grep hit only documentation text in `adv-skill-author`.

### Extends

- `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`: prior research found default status/list/show paths can over-hydrate and become slow. New finding for this change: any active ops/dependency graph read must avoid becoming another full-scan hot path; prefer typed workflow state plus minimal Visibility/search keys and summary readback.
- Archived `enforceCriticalOpsPlanning`: already solved critical in-scope release-safety obligations; new finding: release-first surviving ops obligations remain unsolved and need different semantics from hard blockers.

### Conflict Scan

- Active changes: no direct active duplicate. Related stale drafts exist but do not own ops-follow-up traceability.
- Archived/adjacent: `enforceCriticalOpsPlanning` overlaps required-critical obligations but not release-first linked ops state.
- Validation: `adv_change_validate` passed; `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` are expected before planning/spec deltas.
- Agenda: many pending `subagent-followup` and `required-obligation` items reinforce the core pain: follow-up visibility exists but lacks durable lifecycle linkage.

### Current State

- Same-project fast-follow is child-to-parent only: `FastFollowOfSchema` stores `parent_change_id` and `linked_at` (`plugin/src/types/changes.ts:303-308`).
- Cross-project origin has typed child provenance and source write-back links: `CrossProjectOriginSchema` (`plugin/src/types/changes.ts:198-207`), `CrossProjectLinkSchema` (`plugin/src/types/changes.ts:223-237`), source write-back in `plugin/src/tools/change.ts:854-869`.
- External dependencies exist but are advisory-only by schema: `ExternalDependencySchema` with `advisory: z.literal(true)` (`plugin/src/types/changes.ts:247-270`).
- Required follow-ups from reports become agenda items with source metadata embedded as free text, not typed linkage (`plugin/src/tools/subagent-report.ts:531-584`).
- `adv_change_show` surfaces `_crossProjectOrigin`, `_fastFollowOrigin`, and `_externalDependencyStatus` (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts:2364-2385`).
- `adv_change_list` exposes only fast-follow `parent_change_id` convenience (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts:2079-2081`).
- `adv_wip_state` projects active changes without lineage fields (`plugin/src/tools/backlog.ts:275-285`).
- Search attributes include `AdvBacklogIssueNumber` but no parent/source/ops linkage key; KeywordList budget is explicitly capped at 3 (`plugin/src/temporal/search-attributes.ts:5-28`).

### Edge Cases

1. Parent release-first flow:
   - Parent releases before follow-up can run; link must survive archive and remain visible.
   - Parent has multiple follow-ups with mixed semantics: one blocker, one monitor, one cleanup-after.
2. Required follow-up promotion:
   - Same report follow-up is promoted twice by different agents; collision check must detect duplicate structural provenance.
   - Agenda item predates new schema; promotion must preserve what exists and degrade safely.
3. Evidence log:
   - Backfill runs in batches; batch 3 fails and must be rerun without erasing earlier evidence.
   - Cleanup/teardown is required only after validation passes; state must represent pending cleanup separately from run completion.
4. Visibility/collision:
   - Visibility lag can produce race windows; collision checks must be advisory unless backed by authoritative workflow state.
   - KeywordList budget is full; adding multi-value graph attributes may break local dev-server registration.

### Open Design Questions

1. Relationship vocabulary and edge model
   - Trust model: joint — user-facing labels matter; internal storage can be agent-resolved.
   - Blast radius: wrong labels make release/handoff semantics confusing and collision checks noisy.
   - Alternatives: reuse existing `ExternalDependencyRelationshipSchema`, extend cross-project relationship enum, or create one unified edge enum.
2. Parent release behavior with open ops follow-up
   - Trust model: joint.
   - Blast radius: too strict blocks valid deploy-first ops; too loose recreates hidden follow-up risk.
   - Alternatives: block only `blocks`, explicit handoff for release-first, or warning-only for all non-blocking edges.
3. Evidence-log minimum shape
   - Trust model: joint.
   - Blast radius: too little evidence loses traceability; too much becomes project-management burden.
   - Alternatives: fixed small evidence entry schema, free-form notes plus typed status, or task/checkpoint reuse.
4. Query/index strategy
   - Trust model: agent-owned technical decision.
   - Blast radius: wrong Visibility projection creates slow or eventually inconsistent correctness paths.
   - Alternatives: workflow state + query only, add single Keyword discovery key, or full graph scan. Recommendation: typed workflow state plus at most one single-valued Keyword for discovery.

### Draft Spec Deltas

- `rq-opsFollowTrace01` — Linked ops/enabler follow-up model.
  - Given a follow-up is created from a change/report/agenda source, when it is persisted, then typed source, relationship, status, and provenance are stored in authoritative change workflow state.
- `rq-opsFollowEvidence01` — Lightweight ops evidence log.
  - Given an ops run/batch/monitor/cleanup step occurs, when evidence is recorded, then environment, action, status, timestamp, summary, and next-step/completion signal are captured without requiring full project-manager fields.
- `rq-opsFollowPromotion01` — Follow-up promotion with provenance.
  - Given a report/agenda follow-up is promoted, when the linked work is created, then original change/report/contract/agenda provenance is preserved structurally and duplicate promotion is detected.
- `rq-opsFollowRelease01` — Release-first surviving obligation handoff.
  - Given parent release must occur before follow-up completion, when release is approved, then the linked surviving obligation is shown explicitly and remains queryable after archive.
- `rq-opsFollowWip01` — Planning/collision visibility.
  - Given active linked ops/enabler work exists, when planning or WIP state is read, then relevant active links are surfaced without agenda text search or full-state scan as correctness authority.

### Related Pattern Scan

- Fast-follow lineage: child-to-parent only (`plugin/src/types/changes.ts:303-308`).
- Cross-project linkage: bidirectional source write-back exists (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts:854-869`).
- External dependencies: advisory-only typed model exists (`plugin/src/types/changes.ts:247-270`).
- Required follow-ups: free-text agenda consumer is the lossy chokepoint (`plugin/src/tools/subagent-report.ts:531-584`).
- Backlog claims: `AdvBacklogIssueNumber` shows a precedent for single-value Visibility claim keys without full workflow hydration.

### Completeness Verification

- Problem-completeness: high confidence. Discovery covers proposal lineage, report follow-ups, agenda fallback, status/WIP visibility, search attributes, and prior critical-obligation work.
- Solution-scope: medium-high confidence. Full design remains open, but required surfaces are identified. No unverified sole-entry claim is used.
- Secondary-surface dispositions:
  - In scope: fast-follow/cross-project lineage, required follow-up promotion, WIP/planning visibility, release handoff, specs/tests.
  - Out of scope: general issue/project management, product-specific PokeEdge ops logic, replacing external runbook/incident tools.
  - User-facing unresolved: relationship labels, parent release handoff strength, evidence minimums.

### LBP Check

- Temporal guidance: authoritative dependency/relationship state belongs in workflow state and query APIs; Search Attributes are for discoverability/result sets, not business-logic correctness; memo is not filterable; Visibility is eventually consistent.
- ADV local precedent matches this: typed `ChangeWorkflowState` plus projected search attributes such as `AdvBacklogIssueNumber`.
- Recommendation: keep edges authoritative in typed workflow state, project only minimal discovery keys to Visibility, avoid new KeywordList unless a current one is displaced.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

Auto-adopted low-risk opportunities:

1. Reuse advisory external-dependency/surviving-obligation precedent for release-first follow-ups.
2. Carry typed source-change/report/contract provenance through report-to-promotion path instead of free-text-only agenda notes.
3. Add same-project parent-to-children reverse discoverability, borrowing from cross-project write-back precedent.
4. Store edge graph in typed workflow state and use at most one single-valued Keyword discovery key.

Surfaced to user:

- Relationship vocabulary and `adv_wip_state`/planning read shape need user-facing preference because labels and visibility level affect day-to-day workflow.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

S1 HIGH Completion Signals — Completion proof examples are broad and need user preference for minimum evidence.
  Evidence: "Preserve a lightweight evidence log for run/batch/environment/validation/next-step/cleanup facts."
  Reason: unclear because too little evidence loses traceability, while too much creates bookkeeping.

M1 HIGH Missing Information — Relationship labels and parent release handoff strength remain user-facing decisions.
  Evidence: "Final vocabulary for relationship edges: block, follow, monitor, cleanup-after, teardown-after, rerun-needed, or similar."
  Reason: unclear because relationship labels determine what users and agents see at planning/release time.

Coverage: B:C F:C S:P M:P

Trigger evaluation: two HIGH findings require clarification before discovery completion; no CRITICAL finding.

## Discovery Agenda

### Codebase unknowns

1. Exact current state model for `fast_follow_of`, cross-project origin, change origin, report follow-ups, agenda items, and search attributes. Resolved in Discovery Findings.
2. Best persistence shape: extend `Change` state, add linked-obligation records, add ops-job subtype, or combine typed edge records with normal changes. Partially resolved: authoritative typed workflow state is required; exact object shape deferred to design.
3. Best read paths for agents: `adv_change_show`, `adv_change_list`, `adv_status`, `adv_wip_state`, planning readiness, or a dedicated query. Partially resolved: WIP/planning visibility is in scope; exact UI/query shape deferred to design.
4. How to promote an existing agenda/report follow-up into a linked change or ops-follow-up while preserving provenance. Partially resolved: typed provenance must be preserved; exact promotion tool/API deferred to design.
5. How to store light evidence entries without duplicating task logs or becoming a heavy incident-management system. Needs user preference and design.
6. How failed, partial, rerun-needed, rollback-needed, and cleanup-needed states should be represented and surfaced. Needs user preference and design.

### Ecosystem unknowns

1. Temporal visibility/search attribute limits and query patterns relevant to dependency graph or active ops collision checks. Resolved: workflow state owns correctness; Visibility is advisory/discovery; avoid new KeywordList.
2. Whether existing OpenCode/ADV UX surfaces can show linked operational state clearly without new command complexity. Partially resolved: `adv_change_show`, WIP/planning, and release reports are likely surfaces; exact shape deferred to design.

### Domain unknowns

1. Final vocabulary for relationship edges: block, follow, monitor, cleanup-after, teardown-after, rerun-needed, or similar. User clarification needed.
2. Final completion-proof expectations for common ops patterns: backfill batches, deploy config, monitoring window, cleanup/teardown. User clarification needed.
3. Policy for when parent release requires explicit handoff versus warning versus block. User clarification needed.

### Integration unknowns

1. Interaction with `enforceCriticalOpsPlanning`: resolved direction — critical in-scope obligations remain blockers; release-first follow-ups use explicit surviving-obligation routing.
2. Interaction with backlog coordination and planning collision checks. In scope; design must define query/readback without relying on agenda search.
3. Interaction with cross-project follow-ups and product-linked repositories. In scope where structurally adjacent; product-specific behavior out of scope.
4. Interaction with archive/sign-off reporting: unresolved linked obligations should be surfaced in release/archive report; exact display deferred to design.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Define first-class traceability for ops/enabler follow-ups that need durable ADV linkage.
- Support explicit relationship edges between originating change and linked work.
- Support release-first follow-up semantics where parent release can proceed with explicit surviving obligation.
- Preserve a lightweight evidence log for run/batch/environment/validation/next-step/cleanup facts.
- Make active linked ops/enabler work discoverable during planning and collision checks.
- Define promotion path from report/agenda follow-up into linked tracked work.
- Update relevant specs and tests for the selected model.

### Out of Scope

- Building a general project-management system.
- Replacing GitHub Issues, GitHub Projects, or external incident/runbook tooling.
- Modeling every optional advisory follow-up as a required obligation.
- Making all parent changes wait for all follow-ups before release.
- Implementing product-specific PokeEdge migration logic.
- Designing detailed engineering acceptance criteria before discovery.

### Must Not

- Must not use agenda text search as the correctness authority for active ops obligations.
- Must not rely on heuristic title matching for dependency or collision correctness.
- Must not require humans to maintain links/status manually after agents create the relationship.
- Must not demote required ops/enabler work into untracked backlog notes.
- Must not block release when deployment-first sequencing is explicitly required and a surviving linked obligation is recorded.

# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Preserve important ops/enabler follow-ups as durable linked ADV state instead of agenda-only notes.
2. Support release-first sequencing: parent changes can release while explicit surviving ops obligations remain linked and visible.
3. Provide light structured operational evidence so agents can resume, validate, rerun, or clean up follow-up work without confusion.
4. Surface active linked ops/enabler work during planning and WIP/collision checks.
5. Preserve existing required-critical release-safety enforcement while adding better routing for non-blocking post-release ops obligations.

## Success Criteria

1. Agents can find active ops/enabler follow-ups from relevant ADV state without agenda archaeology.
2. Parent changes can release with explicit surviving ops obligations when release-first sequencing is required.
3. Ops follow-up state is clear enough for agents to know whether work is not started, running/partial, failed, rerun-needed, cleanup-needed, or complete.
4. Required in-scope release-safety obligations remain blocking; release-first follow-ups become explicit linked obligations, not silent backlog notes.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Linked ops/enabler work persists structural source provenance: originating change/report/agenda source, relationship, status, and timestamp in authoritative workflow state.
2. Report/agenda follow-up promotion preserves typed provenance; free-text agenda descriptions are not the sole source of truth.
3. Light evidence entries support env, action/batch, status, timestamp, evidence summary, and next step or completion signal.
4. Evidence state supports partial, failed, rerun-needed, rollback/cleanup-needed, and complete outcomes.
5. Parent release/archive reporting surfaces open linked ops obligations and records explicit handoff for non-blocking release-first work.
6. Blocking linked obligations prevent release; non-blocking release-first obligations do not block once explicit handoff is recorded.
7. Planning/WIP collision checks surface active linked ops work from structural state or Visibility-backed discovery, not agenda text search.
8. Same-project and cross-project follow-up links support finding child/follow-up work from the parent/source context.
9. Existing required-critical obligation enforcement from `enforceCriticalOpsPlanning` remains intact.
10. Specs and tests cover linked provenance, promotion, evidence state, release handoff, planning/WIP visibility, and backward compatibility.

## Constraints

1. Do not turn ADV into a project-manager clone.
2. Do not make every linked ops follow-up a release blocker.
3. Do not rely on agenda scanning as the source of truth.
4. Do not require active human bookkeeping.
5. Do not make the model infra-specific; it must cover migrations, backfills, deploy config, monitoring, cleanup, teardown, docs, and similar enablers.
6. Preserve structural correctness: typed state and validated transitions over title/priority heuristics.
7. Preserve existing human checkpoints and approval boundaries.
8. Keep the operational evidence record light but durable.
9. Relationship labels are agent-context labels; exact enum names are design-owned and do not need to optimize for user-facing copy.
10. Design must evaluate whether a runbook-like model helps without creating heavyweight bookkeeping.

## Avoidances

1. Do not use agenda text search as the correctness authority for active ops obligations.
2. Do not rely on heuristic title matching for dependency or collision correctness.
3. Do not require humans to maintain links/status manually after agents create the relationship.
4. Do not demote required ops/enabler work into untracked backlog notes.
5. Do not block release when deployment-first sequencing is explicitly required and a surviving linked obligation is recorded.
6. Do not add new KeywordList search attributes unless an existing KeywordList is intentionally displaced.
7. Do not use Temporal Memo for filterable relationship/collision data.

## Out of Scope

1. Building a general project-management system.
2. Replacing GitHub Issues, GitHub Projects, or external incident/runbook tooling.
3. Modeling every optional advisory follow-up as a required obligation.
4. Making all parent changes wait for all follow-ups before release.
5. Implementing product-specific PokeEdge migration logic.
6. Designing or running product-specific migrations/backfills.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: false

Rationale: this change affects ADV workflow state, tools, terminal/status/readback surfaces, and specs. It does not affect browser-visible UI or frontend component rendering.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. Relationship labels may be finalized in design; they are agent context, not critical user-facing copy.
2. Parent release behavior: use explicit handoff for open non-blocking/release-first linked ops obligations.
3. Evidence minimum: use light structured evidence; design should consider runbook-shaped representation if it stays lightweight.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. Authoritative relationship state belongs in typed change workflow state and query/readback paths.
2. Temporal Visibility may provide minimal discovery keys, but must not be the sole correctness authority because it is eventually consistent.
3. Avoid new KeywordList search attributes; current local dev-server KeywordList budget is full.
4. Preserve existing required-critical obligation behavior as the hard-blocking path for in-scope release-safety work.
5. Reuse or align with existing cross-project/external-dependency primitives where possible instead of introducing parallel linkage machinery.

## Deferred Questions

1. Exact relationship enum names and readback labels — deferred to `/adv-design`.
2. Whether evidence entries should explicitly use a runbook-shaped schema — deferred to `/adv-design`.

## Sign-Off

Approved by user reply: `approve`.

# Design

## Architecture Overview

Add ops/enabler follow-up traceability by extending the existing change-workflow state model instead of creating a separate project-management object.

Core model:

1. **Ops follow-up remains a normal ADV change** when it needs ADV-level rigor. It keeps the seven-gate lifecycle, artifacts, tasks, review, and archive record, plus a lightweight `ops_followup` profile.
2. **Relationship edges are typed workflow state.** Parent/source changes record outbound follow-up links; child/follow-up changes record inbound source provenance and operational evidence.
3. **Evidence is append-only lightweight ops context.** Entries capture environment, action/batch, status, timestamp, summary, and next step or completion signal.
4. **Visibility/Search Attributes are discovery aids only.** Authoritative correctness lives in workflow state/query paths. Visibility may project at most a minimal single-valued Keyword key; no new KeywordList unless an existing one is intentionally retired.
5. **Release-first obligations use explicit handoff.** Blocking edges block release. Non-blocking release-first edges do not block after release report records explicit surviving-obligation handoff.

This preserves existing required-critical planning enforcement through `checkCriticalOpsCoverage` / `rq-PR007coc`: in-scope required-critical release-safety obligations remain blockers. The new model handles linked release-first or post-release operational follow-ups that should survive parent release without becoming agenda archaeology.

## Key Decisions

### 1. Use full ADV changes for rigorous ops follow-ups

Linked ops/enabler work is a normal ADV change with an ops profile, not a standalone lightweight job object.

Rationale: full changes already provide gates, artifacts, tasks, contracts, review, archive, and crash recovery. A separate ops-job lifecycle would duplicate these semantics and drift into project-management scope.

### 2. Add `ops_followup` profile to the child/follow-up change

Proposed typed shape:

```ts
interface OpsFollowupProfile {
  kind: "migration" | "backfill" | "deploy_config" | "monitoring" | "cleanup" | "teardown" | "other";
  source: OpsFollowupSource;
  relationship: OpsRelationship;
  status: OpsFollowupStatus;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at?: string;
  completion_signal?: string;
  evidence: OpsEvidenceEntry[];
}

type OpsRelationship = "blocks" | "follows_release" | "monitors" | "cleanup_after";
type OpsFollowupStatus = "not_started" | "running" | "partial" | "failed" | "rerun_needed" | "rollback_needed" | "cleanup_needed" | "complete";
```

`OpsRelationship` is the single ops relationship vocabulary. Existing `CrossProjectLinkRelationshipSchema`, `ExternalDependencyRelationshipSchema`, and `fast_follow_of` remain backward-compatible and map into this read model where applicable.

Relationship semantics:

- `blocks`: must finish before parent release/acceptance.
- `follows_release`: parent must release first; child remains surviving obligation.
- `monitors`: observation/validation window after release/run.
- `cleanup_after`: cleanup/teardown after validation or run completion.

### 3. Split authority: parent owns release edge; child owns evidence/status

Validator caution resolved here.

Authoritative ownership:

- **Parent/source `ops_followup_links[]`** owns edge existence for parent release/archive reporting and parent-context discovery after successful promotion.
- **Child `ops_followup.source`** owns repairable provenance mirror and child-context readback.
- **Child `ops_followup.evidence[]` and `ops_followup.status`** own operational progress/evidence.
- Parent `ops_followup_links[].status` is a compact last-known display snapshot only, not the status source of truth.

Promotion is successful only after child profile and parent/source link are both written or after the tool returns a typed partial-link diagnostic with repair guidance. If source-link write fails after child creation, child provenance is sufficient for a repair/retry path.

Proposed link shape:

```ts
interface OpsFollowupLink {
  id: string;
  target_project_id?: string;
  target_path?: string;
  changeId: string;
  relationship: OpsRelationship;
  status: OpsFollowupStatus; // last-known display snapshot
  required_handoff: boolean;
  linked_at: string;
  source_artifact?: string;
  source_contract_id?: string;
}
```

### 4. Promote from typed follow-up source, not agenda text

Promotion starts from the most structured source available:

1. `required_follow_ups[]` / typed sub-agent report payload (`RequiredFollowUp` with `obligation_class`, `severity`, `source_contract_id`).
2. Sidecar sub-agent report metadata (`change_id`, `scope`, `agent`, `attempt`, optional task ID).
3. Agenda item ID only as legacy/fallback source.
4. Manual source only when no report/agenda artifact exists.

Candidate tool surface:

- `adv_followup_promote`
  - Inputs: source change ID, source kind (`required_follow_up`, `report_follow_up`, `agenda`, `manual`), source artifact ID, relationship, ops kind, summary/capability, optional target project path.
  - Creates linked ADV child change with `ops_followup` profile.
  - Writes reverse link to parent/source change.
  - Preserves report/agenda/contract provenance structurally.
  - Detects duplicate promotions from source identity, not title matching.

Existing agenda item creation can remain as compatibility/readability fallback, but agenda text must not be authority.

### 5. Add evidence append/update tool for ops profile

Candidate tool surface:

- `adv_ops_evidence_add`
  - Inputs: change ID, environment, action/batch, status, summary, next step, completion signal.
  - Appends an `OpsEvidenceEntry` and updates child profile status.
  - Uses signal + refresh helper, same cache discipline as task/gate tools.

Proposed evidence shape:

```ts
interface OpsEvidenceEntry {
  id: string;
  recorded_at: string;
  env: string;
  action: string;
  batch?: string;
  status: "started" | "partial" | "pass" | "fail" | "rerun_needed" | "rollback_needed" | "cleanup_needed" | "complete";
  summary: string;
  next_step?: string;
  completion_signal?: string;
}
```

This is runbook-shaped but not a full runbook system: enough for continuation, validation, rerun, and cleanup.

### 6. Planning/WIP visibility uses structural read paths

Expose compact ops annotations in:

- `adv_change_show`: inbound profile + outbound links.
- `adv_change_list`: compact parent/source/ops annotations.
- `adv_wip_state`: compact active ops follow-up annotations in `active_changes`.
- Planning/collision checks: structural state or minimal Visibility key; never agenda text search.

Read-path constraint from `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`: avoid full hydration in hot list/status/WIP paths. Memo may carry display data but not filter/collision authority.

### 7. Release and archive reporting

- `blocks`: release gate blocked until child status is complete or relationship is changed through explicit approved routing.
- `follows_release`, `monitors`, `cleanup_after`: release can proceed only after release report shows linked surviving obligation and records explicit handoff.
- Archive/sign-off report lists open linked ops follow-ups with relationship, child change ID, status, and last evidence summary.

## ADR Drafts

No ADR required. This extends established ADV Temporal typed-state + signal/query + Visibility projection patterns.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Types and schemas**
   - Add `OpsFollowupProfile`, `OpsFollowupSource`, `OpsFollowupLink`, `OpsEvidenceEntry`, `OpsRelationship`, and `OpsFollowupStatus` Zod schemas near `plugin/src/types/changes.ts`.
   - Add optional fields to `ChangeSchema`.
   - Add legacy normalization for missing fields.
2. **Workflow state and signals**
   - Seed/create child ops profile at promotion/create time.
   - Add signal(s) for evidence append/status update.
   - Add signal/save-path support for parent/source outbound links.
   - Preserve signal-only workflow surface; no `defineUpdate`.
3. **Promotion tool**
   - Implement `adv_followup_promote` or equivalent wrapper around `adv_change_create`.
   - Prefer typed `RequiredFollowUp` / sidecar report source over agenda text.
   - Validate source artifacts where structurally available.
   - Duplicate check by source artifact + source change + relationship.
   - Return typed partial-link diagnostic if child creation succeeds but source link fails.
4. **Evidence tool**
   - Implement `adv_ops_evidence_add` or equivalent focused tool.
   - Append evidence entries; update child ops status; refresh cache.
5. **Read surfaces**
   - Extend `adv_change_show`, `adv_change_list`, and `adv_wip_state` with compact annotations.
6. **Release/harden checks**
   - Add logic for blocking links and explicit surviving-obligation handoff.
   - Preserve `checkCriticalOpsCoverage` behavior unchanged.
7. **Specs and tests**
   - Update advance-workflow, subagent-reports, backlog-coordination/WIP, and prep-readiness as needed.
   - Test promotion, evidence append, release handoff, WIP visibility, duplicate promotion, repair from child provenance, and legacy compatibility.

## LBP Analysis

Chosen approach matches ADV long-term practice:

- Typed schemas own correctness.
- Workflow state is authoritative.
- Signals mutate state; queries/read tools expose state.
- Search Attributes support cross-workflow discovery, not correctness.
- Existing seven-gate changes remain lifecycle unit.

Rejected alternatives:

1. Agenda-only enhancement — agenda text cannot own correctness or durable lineage.
2. Separate ops-job lifecycle — duplicates change lifecycle/archive/review semantics.
3. Visibility-first dependency graph — Visibility is eventually consistent and KeywordList budget is constrained.
4. Free-form runbook artifact only — agents need structured status and collision visibility.
5. Reusing `ExternalDependencySchema` unchanged for blockers — schema is `advisory: true`, conflicting with blocking relationship requirements.

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/types/changes.ts`
- `plugin/src/types/signals.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/change-state.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/workflows.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/messages.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/search-attributes.ts` if minimal Keyword projection is needed
- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/subagent-report.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/agenda.ts` for optional typed source metadata fallback
- `plugin/src/tools/backlog.ts` / WIP state
- `plugin/src/tools/gate.ts` and gate-readiness helpers
- `plugin/src/validator/prep-readiness.ts` to preserve `checkCriticalOpsCoverage` / `rq-PR007coc`
- `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json`
- `.adv/specs/subagent-reports/spec.json`
- `.adv/specs/backlog-coordination/spec.json`
- `.adv/specs/prep-readiness/spec.json` if planning checks are added there

## Design-Derived Criteria

1. No new KeywordList search attribute may be added unless a test or migration removes/replaces an existing KeywordList.
2. Visibility-derived collision findings are advisory unless backed by authoritative workflow state/query validation.
3. Ops evidence entries are append-only; child profile status may update to latest current state.
4. Promotion duplicate detection uses structural source identity, not title matching.
5. Hot read surfaces expose compact annotations without full artifact hydration.
6. Existing changes without ops fields parse and read successfully.
7. Existing `fast_follow_of`, `cross_project_origin`, `cross_project_links`, and `external_dependencies` behavior remains backward-compatible.
8. Promotion from sub-agent output consumes typed `RequiredFollowUp` / report metadata when available before agenda text fallback.
9. Blocking semantics must not be implemented by reusing `ExternalDependencySchema` unchanged.
10. Parent/source reverse-link partial failures are repairable from child `ops_followup.source` provenance.
11. Agreement AC9 is interpreted as preserving the existing required-critical enforcement implemented by `checkCriticalOpsCoverage` / `rq-PR007coc`; the old `enforceCriticalOpsPlanning` wording is a naming drift, not a semantic change.

## Risks / Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
| --- | --- |
| Model grows into project-management system | Keep ops profile fields minimal; evidence entries capture continuation facts only. |
| Duplicate relationship vocabularies | Define one `OpsRelationship` enum and map existing relationship fields into it. |
| Visibility race creates false collision confidence | Treat Visibility as discovery/advisory; enforce duplicates by source identity in workflow state/tool validation. |
| Parent/source reverse link write fails after child create | Child structural provenance is sufficient for repair/retry; promotion returns typed partial-link diagnostic. |
| Release-first handoff becomes hidden again | Require release/archive report to list surviving linked obligations before release completion. |
| Legacy changes fail parsing | Optional fields + boundary normalization + backward-compat tests. |
| Advisory dependency primitive reused incorrectly | Tests assert blocking ops links are not stored as `ExternalDependencySchema` with `advisory: true`. |

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout considered 5 candidates.

Incorporated:

1. Promotion consumes typed `RequiredFollowUp` / sidecar report metadata before agenda text fallback.
2. Use one ops relationship enum and mapping layer instead of adding a fourth unrelated vocabulary.
3. Reject using `ExternalDependencySchema` unchanged for blocking ops because it is advisory-only.
4. Correct required-critical enforcement anchor to `checkCriticalOpsCoverage` / `rq-PR007coc`.
5. Specify repair from child provenance for parent/source reverse-link partial failure.

Surfaced to user: none. All adopted items preserve agreement and do not introduce user-value tradeoffs.

## Validator Result

Validator: `VALIDATED` with cautions.

Cautions handled:

1. Agreement AC9 names old/nonexistent `enforceCriticalOpsPlanning`; design records correct implementation anchor `checkCriticalOpsCoverage` / `rq-PR007coc` and treats this as naming drift only.
2. Source-of-truth for link authority was underspecified; design now splits authority: parent/source owns edge existence for release/discovery, child owns provenance mirror plus status/evidence, and partial writes are repairable from child provenance.

No unresolved `CONFLICT`. No contract-compromise risk identified.

# Executive Summary

Implemented durable ADV ops/enabler follow-up traceability.

## What changed

- Added typed ops follow-up state to changes: child `ops_followup` profiles, parent `ops_followup_links`, source provenance, relationship, status, evidence, and release-handoff metadata.
- Added workflow signals/reducers/projections for seeding follow-up profiles, linking parent/child changes, and appending operational evidence.
- Added `adv_followup_promote` to create linked ops follow-up ADV changes from typed report/agenda/manual provenance without title matching or agenda-text authority.
- Added `adv_ops_evidence_add` to append lightweight operational evidence and update follow-up status.
- Added compact readback to `adv_change_show`, `adv_change_list`, and `adv_wip_state` so agents can discover active linked ops work structurally.
- Added release enforcement and archive/reporting support: blocking links block release; non-blocking release-first obligations survive through explicit handoff/open-obligation reporting.
- Updated specs/docs/schema artifacts and tests for provenance, promotion, evidence, release handoff, WIP visibility, and backward compatibility.

## Verification

- `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/subagent-reports-spec-assets.test.ts src/validator/clarify-readiness.e2e.test.ts` passed.
- `bin/oc-test full` passed.
- `pnpm run check` passed.
- `pnpm run build` passed.
- Independent acceptance reviewer verdict: READY, 0 blocking findings, 0 nonblocking findings; reviewer targeted 68 tests passed.

## Remaining concerns

None known.
